mechanism_narrative: The level of retail sales (RSAFS) reflects consumer spending momentum; above-trend readings signal persistent demand that drives corporate earnings and lifts equity prices over the very near term.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year correlation of RSAFS level with forward 5-day returns is not positive (sign test p > 0.10) or the average return in top-quartile RSAFS months is not greater than in bottom-quartile.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is substantially above the neutral rate, it tightens financial conditions, raises discount rates, and depresses equity valuations; a level above 4.5% persistently signals a restrictive stance.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, the average 20-day SPX return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deterioration in both payroll employment (PAYEMS falling) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE rising) indicates a weakening labor market that reduces consumer income and corporate profits, leading to negative equity returns over a quarter ahead.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change > 0.2 pp and PAYEMS 3-month change < 0
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the 60-day SPX return is positive in more than 40% of occurrences over any 5-year window, rejecting the directional prediction.

mechanism_narrative: During demand-driven expansions (GDPC1 YoY growth > 3%), rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy capacity utilization and supports earnings, making further equity gains likely. The effect is conditional on demand strength.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 YoY > 3%, the rolling 2-year correlation of INDPRO growth with forward 20-day returns is not positive (one-sided p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) erodes real purchasing power, forces monetary tightening expectations, and lowers equity valuations; the effect is persistent and does not require a threshold.
failure_condition: The average 60-day SPX return in months with year-on-year CPI above its 12-month median is not lower than in months below median (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of residential investment; a sharp drop below 1,200 thousand units signals a housing downturn that drags on economic activity and corporate earnings, negative for equities in the short run.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1,200, the proportion of positive 5-day SPX returns exceeds 50% (binomial test p < 0.10) over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%) but the policy rate is still below 4%, real rates are deeply negative, prompting markets to anticipate aggressive future tightening, which depresses equity prices over the next month.
regime_hint: low real rates with high inflation
failure_condition: In any 3-year period where the trigger condition occurs at least 6 times, the median 20-day SPX return is not negative (Wilcoxon signed-rank test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In an extremely tight labor market (UNRATE below 4% and monthly payroll gains exceeding 200k), wage inflation pressures rise, squeezing corporate margins and forcing the Fed to maintain or raise rates, producing negative equity returns over a quarter.
regime_hint: tight labor market (UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS growth > 200k)
failure_condition: During regimes meeting the trigger condition, the average 60-day SPX return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year walk-forward period.
